response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web, backend, and open-source credentials, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. Professional C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the bulk of the career is TypeScript/Node.js web development, with systems-level work in DLang rather than C++. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL practice, MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, or the listed OT protocols, making the fit weak for a senior C++ role in automation machinery.
strengths: High-performance, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby, handling millions of requests/sec
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring and workshop/teaching track record
Broad full-stack range (backend, web, mobile, embedded touchpoints) with long open-source project ownership
concerns: Minimal professional C++ (one short role in 2015); systems work is in DLang, not the required C++/STL stack
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3) experience
Career trajectory centers on web/product engineering, not networked multi-threaded C++ platform architecture

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or automation domain background
Proven low-latency distributed-systems and performance engineering skills
